name: Duwende
description: Duwendes are earth spirits or goblins in Philippine mythology. They are often depicted as small, human-like creatures inhabiting houses, forests, and anthills. They can be either good or bad, depending on how humans treat them. A respectful attitude towards nature and offering them food is believed to appease them, while disturbing their homes or showing disrespect can provoke their wrath. Duwendes are known for their mischievous nature, playing pranks on humans, hiding objects, and causing minor disturbances. However, they can also be benevolent, bringing good luck and fortune to those who are respectful. Some duwendes are believed to be guardians of treasure, while others are simply territorial spirits protecting their homes. They are an integral part of Filipino folklore and are a reminder to respect the natural world and the spirits that inhabit it. Offerings such as food, coins, or shiny objects are often left for them in hopes of gaining their favor or avoiding their mischief. They are often described as having a connection to the land and a deep understanding of its secrets.
subtitle: Mischievous earth spirits of the Philippines
appearance: Duwendes are generally described as small, humanoid creatures, often resembling miniature old men or children. They vary in appearance, some having brown or black skin, while others are described as having pale or even glowing skin. They often wear simple clothing, such as leaves, bark, or small pieces of cloth. Some are said to have pointed ears and large heads. Their size typically ranges from a few inches to a foot tall. They are sometimes depicted with beards or wrinkles, emphasizing their age and connection to the earth.
habitat: Duwendes are believed to inhabit a variety of places, including houses (especially old ones), forests, anthills, under large trees, and in gardens. They are often associated with places that are considered to be untouched by humans or that have a strong connection to nature. They are also believed to live in the basements or attics of houses, where they can observe the human inhabitants without being seen.
behavior: Duwendes are known for their mischievous and playful behavior. They often play pranks on humans, such as hiding objects, making noises, and causing minor inconveniences. However, they can also be helpful, bringing good luck and fortune to those who are respectful. They are territorial and protective of their homes, and they will often punish those who disturb them or show disrespect. They are sensitive to human behavior and will react accordingly, rewarding kindness with kindness and punishing disrespect with mischief.

keyMyths.title: The Lost Comb
keyMyths.description: A girl loses her comb in the forest and accuses a duwende of stealing it. The duwende, angered by the accusation, causes her to fall ill until she apologizes and offers a gift.

keyMyths.title: The Helpful Duwende
keyMyths.description: A farmer treats a duwende with kindness and respect, offering it food and shelter. In return, the duwende helps the farmer with his crops, ensuring a bountiful harvest.
